prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. Argentina's debt restructuring initiative commenced on January 14, 2005. This process enabled the nation to restart payments on 76% of its US$82 billion in sovereign bonds, which had gone into default in 2001 amidst the most severe economic crisis the country had ever experienced. A subsequent debt restructuring, implemented in 2010, elevated the proportion of bonds under some arrangement of repayment to 93%, notwithstanding persistent disagreements with holdout creditors. Bondholders who took part in this restructuring accepted repayments amounting to approximately 30% of the face value and agreed to deferred payment schedules; subsequently, they began to receive their payments promptly. Concurrently, the value of their previously almost worthless bonds started to appreciate. The outstanding 7% of bondholders eventually received 25% less than their original demands, following the actions of a centre-right, US-aligned leader.
B. The Argentine debt restructuring, which began on January 14, 2005, allowed the country to recoup 76% of the US$82 billion in sovereign bonds that defaulted in 2001 during the worst economic crisis in the country's history. A second debt restructuring in 2010 brought the percentage of bonds under some form of repayment to 93%, although there were still ongoing disputes with holdouts. Bondholders who participated in the restructuring settled for repayments of approximately 30% of face value and deferred payment terms, and started to be paid promptly. The remaining bondholders were ultimately repaid 25% less than 7% of
C. The Argentine debt restructuring is a process of debt restructuring by Argentina that began on January 14, 2005, and allowed it to resume payment on 76% of the US$82 billion in sovereign bonds that defaulted in 2001 at the depth of the worst economic crisis in the nation's history. A second debt restructuring in 2010 brought the percentage of bonds under some form of repayment to 93%, though ongoing disputes with holdouts remained. Bondholders who participated in the restructuring settled for repayments of around 30% of face value and deferred payment terms, and began to be paid punctually; the value of their nearly worthless bonds also began to rise. The remaining 7% of bondholders were later repaid 25% less then they were demanding, after centre-right and US-aligned leader
